title: Crispy Masala Peanuts
description: Spiced roasted peanuts with a light besan crust. Classic tea-time muncher and craving snack.
explanation: Masala peanuts hit the salty-spicy craving without a deep fryer if you roast or air-fry carefully. A thin besan coating gives the street-style crunch.
Portion-aware: peanuts are calorie-dense, so a small bowl with chai is better than endless grazing from a big jar.
instructions: Mix besan, rice flour, spices, salt, baking soda, oil, and just enough water for a thick paste.
Coat peanuts evenly in the paste.
Spread on a tray. Bake at 180°C for 15–20 minutes, stirring once, until golden and crisp—or air-fry at 170°C for 10–12 minutes.
stepNotes: Thin coating crisps better than thick clumps.
Stir once mid-bake so undersides do not stay soft.
They crisp further as they cool—do not overbake to dark brown.
Pan method: roast slowly on low heat with constant stirring (harder but possible).
Cool before sealing the jar or steam softens them.

variationSections.title: Spice routes
items: Garlic powder and oregano for a pizza note.
Curry leaves powder and black pepper.

variationSections.title: Lighter option
items: Skip besan and dry-roast peanuts with spices only.
Mix half peanuts with roasted chana to lower calorie density.
